title: Looking for assistant project manager (UK) with Monday skills
excerpt: Hello! We are a start-up company that is designing and launching new multi-sport venues across the UK!  We are currently hiring an assistant project manager and we’d love to find someone that is really good with Monday. …

posts.author: Sam-H (Sam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! We are a start-up company that is designing and launching new multi-sport venues across the UK! We are currently hiring an assistant project manager and we’d love to find someone that is really good with Monday. We’re based in London, but open to applications from across the UK.
Our website is here: https://www.socialsportssociety.com/
The job advert is here: UK: Assistant Project Manager with Social Sports Society | Escape The City
We use Monday as our main project tool, but we are generalist / basic users. As part of this role, we’d love to fine someone that can uplevel our use of Monday and take our project efficiency to the next level. Many thanks!

posts.author: DamcoSolutions ( Neelam Bhatt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Sam-H

Choosing monday.com as your project tool is a worthwhile decision! Though implementation is easier, it requires a strategic approach to build boards and workflows using monday.com. By considering the objectives, scope, and milestones of your business during board creation, you can minimize time-consuming activities. It’s one of the best ways to bring big automation to life and modernize processes without overeating resources.

If you’re looking to maximize the potential of monday.com, hiring monday.com consultants from a reputable partner company is the right choice. They can build and set up exclusive boards in line with your project, process, or business objectives.
